title: The Utility of Isolation Hospitals in Diminishing the Spread of Scarlet Fever
author: Newsholme, Arthur
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-zero
description: There have not been wanting those who have alleged that isolation hospitals have failed to fulfil the object for which they were erected, inasmuch as scarlet fever has during the last few years been prevalent to an exceptional extent. Those who argue thus, are in favour of diminishing expenditure on the erection and maintenance of isolation hospitals, and consider that we must trust to "improved sanitation" for diminishing and possibly in the end annihilating infectious diseases.
steps_raw: There have not been wanting those who have alleged that isolation hospitals have failed to fulfil the object for which they were erected, inasmuch as scarlet fever has during the last few years been prevalent to an exceptional extent. Those who argue thus, are in favour of diminishing expenditure on the erection and maintenance of isolation hospitals, and consider that we must trust to "improved sanitation" for diminishing and possibly in the end annihilating infectious diseases.
